artifact.name: BD_02_FOUNDATION_SPEC_LOCK_REPORT.json
artifact.title: Current-user, tenant, role and object-scope foundation

locked_decisions.decision: DB-user JWT CurrentUserContext is the canonical source for P0 process/API identity.
locked_decisions.effect: Demo-session helpers are allowed as local fixtures and legacy adapters, but not as final P0 permission truth.

locked_decisions.decision: Route shell, action authority, payload visibility and object scope are separate proof layers.
locked_decisions.effect: Future tickets cannot claim completion from route rendering or visible UI alone.

locked_decisions.decision: Admin and Security are governance/non-bypass roles, not superuser release roles.
locked_decisions.effect: They may manage access controls but cannot force advisor approval, compliance release, evidence sufficiency, client visibility or export.

bold_cleanup_recommendations_to_carry_fo: Move new P0 process APIs to CurrentUserContext and use a narrow adapter only for legacy DemoSession services.
Deprecate createDemoSession in safety-critical tests; use requireDemoSession or DB/JWT current-user context.
Make reasonCode, no-payload, no-mutation and no-release assertions mandatory for negative access tests.
Treat query-param roleKey/tenantSlug APIs as demo/readmodel or migration-needed until they are behind current-user context.
remaining_limits: BD-02 is a foundation/spec-lock ticket; it does not migrate legacy query-param APIs.
BD-02 does not implement the evidence lifecycle, internal draft leak gate, client projection route proof, audit persistence spine or export package redaction.
Coverage gaps are explicitly assigned forward to BD-04, BD-06, BD-09, BD-10, BD-11 and BD-12.
